name: Plantain Chips
intro: The ripeness of plantain aﬀects its flavour. Here we will use green/unripe plantain for a more savoury flavour profile. For a sweeter version, you want to go for just- ripened but firm plantain. Although this recipe calls for frying to give you the original cooking method for this snack, you can also bake them for a healthier option (baked recipe on my website vegannigerian.com).

ingredients.item: sunflower or vegetable oil for deep- frying
sugar (optional) 1/2 teaspoon chilli powder Pinch of salt (optional)

method: Heat the sunflower oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Fry the plantain strips in small batches in the hot oil until golden brown and crispy. This should take about 3-5 minutes. It is important not to overcrowd the pan so that the oil remains hot.

Place the plantain in a mixing bowl and use a fork to mash until you have a lumpy puree. Add the flour, yeast, sugar, salt, chilli and water. Mix well until you have a thick batter. Cover the bowl with a cloth and leave to rise in a warm place for 45 minutes. After this time, gently mix the batter to remove the air bubbles.

Heat the sunflower oil in a small non-stick saucepan over medium-high heat. When hot, add the plantain mixture in round dollops using a tablespoon. Fry, turning them over a few times to brown on all sides. The mosa should float to the top of the oil. Use a slotted spoon to transfer to a plate or tray lined with kitchen paper, to drain oﬀ any excess oil. Leave to cool before serving.
